title: quercus ilex subvar. laurifolia
intro: Quercus ilex subvar. laurifolia, also known as the golden or laurel-leafed holm oak, is a subspecies of the holm oak tree (Quercus ilex) that is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its rich content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds, which have been shown to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The medicinal properties of Quercus ilex subvar. laurifolia have been associated with various health benefits, including the reduction of inflammation and oxidative stress, and the improvement of cardiovascular health and immune function. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is sometimes used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
